titleStmt.title: SII 40.158: original edition by K. Munirathnam
No. 158. (A.R. No. B. 292 of 1931.) Chinna-Garlapāḍu, Palnad Taluk, Guntur District, Andhra Pradesh.

summary.p: On a slab set up in front of the Āñjanēya temple in the village.
This is dated Śaka 1731, Vibhava, Śrāvaṇa, śu. 15.

Records the reconstruction of the temple Lakshmī-Chennakēśava at Chinagārlapāḍu and also gift of land for providing services by Kaḷḷēpalli Subbarāyuḍu, the Samuddār (of the village), when Akumannāṭi Narasayya and Kandāḷam Śrīnivāsācharya were administering (the district), under the Kumpinivāru (The East India Company).

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī jayābhyudaya śālivāhana śakava¨rṣaṁ
bbulu 1731 Agunēṭi vibhavanāma saṁvatsara śrāvaṇa śuddha
15 lu śrīmadakhilāṁḍakōṭi braṁhmāṁḍḍa nāyaku
layni taṁggeḍa paragaṇālō cērina duṁ . cnigā¨rlapā
ṭilō pūrvaṁ vēṁcēśi vuṁḍḍē śrī lakṣmī ceṁnnakēśa
va svāmivāru Anēka saṁvatsaramulanuṁcci
naivēdya dīpārādhanalu lēka khilamaivuṁḍḍagā ha
rabbal kuṁppini harihayāmilō Āku
mannāṭi narsayyagāri vyavahāraṁlō kaṁdā
ḷaṁ śrīnivāsācāryulagāri Āmallō
śśākhādhyāyulunnu Āśvalāyana sūtṛlu
viśvāmitra gōtrōdbhavulunnu śītārāmu
lagāri pautṛlunnu vāsudēvarāyanigāri pu
tṛluṁnaina kaḷlēpalli subbarāyaḍu samuddāri vyavahāracena
vuṁḍē svāmivāriki punaha pratiṣṭa cēyiṁci sarkāru
vāri śelavu prakāraṁ 0 . majukūri kāpulu A
nnapareḍḍi muttāreḍḍi vagairālu karaṇālu A
bbarāju pāpayya vagairālatō ceppi nityanai
vēdya dīpārādhana modalaina kaiṁkaryamulaku 1 .
majukūrilō naḍicē meṭṭapolaṁ reṁḍu kuccaḷu
māgāṇi polaṁ krayaṁ muttuṁ vittanāla
paṭṭu 2-0-0 polamunnu sama¨rpiṁśtimi ga
nku yī kaikaryamu Ācaṁdrā¨rkkasthāyigā
nu viḍcina śāsanaṁ vadhariṁccavalenu . ślōkaḥ .
madvaṁśajāḥ paramahīpativaṁśajāvāḥ
yē bhūmipāḥ satata mujvala dharma
cittāḥ . maddharmamēva paripālana mā
daraṁtti . tatpādakadvayamahaṁ śi
rasāddahāmi . śrī śrī śrī
śrī śrī
Avukuḍu mārapalli kṛṣṇamācā¨ryyulu
Digital edition of SII 40.158 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
